summary: Iter 57 — Nano Banana AI image render for share card. Backend: 6/6 NEW tests PASS — 401 no-auth, 400 invalid MIME (text/plain rejected with Turkish error), 413 oversize >10MB, 422 unknown style (Pydantic Literal validation), 200 studio_dark (24.4s, mime=image/jpeg, b64=612,100 chars), 200 dramatic_lighting (31.4s, b64=771,272 chars). Regression iter50+51+52+55: 22/22 PASS. Iter56 validation re-check: 4/4 PASS. Total backend = 28/28 PASS (6 new + 22 regression). Frontend e2e: Login → /araclar (5 cars listed) → vehicle-menu-* dropdown → share-<carId> opens ShareCardModal. share-format-row visible with all 3 format buttons (classic, story_9_16, square_1_1). ai-render-panel visible with all 4 style buttons (studio_dark, dramatic_lighting, billboard, showroom) + ai-render-btn + share-card-download-btn + share-card-copy-text-btn. Format switching VERIFIED: aspect-ratio changed 1080/1458 → 1080/1920 on Story click. ai-render-btn correctly DISABLED when originalPhotoUrl is null (car had no photo) — UX prevents calling the LLM without input, which is even better than firing an error toast.

integration_issues.issue: After clicking share-card-copy-text-btn, no sonner toast detected (queried [data-sonner-toast], li[role=status], [class*=toast] within 1.5s). Likely clipboard API blocked in preview iframe context — toast.error('Kopyalanamadı') may have fired and auto-dismissed, or sonner <Toaster /> mount needs verification. Non-blocking for iter57 AI render feature, but worth a sanity check.

issues: Radix DialogContent emits accessibility warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined}' — add <DialogDescription> for a11y compliance.

passed_tests: POST /api/ai/render-car 400 invalid MIME (text/plain rejected with Turkish 'tür/jpg/png' error)
POST /api/ai/render-car 422 unknown style 'neon_cyberpunk' rejected (Pydantic Literal validation)
POST /api/ai/render-car 200 style=studio_dark — 24.4s LLM round-trip, mime=image/jpeg, image_base64=612,100 chars
Regression iter50 — 12/12 PASS (health, sold-by, batch tx, satis-role 403, perms version, migration idempotency)
Regression iter56 validation — 4/4 PASS (OCR auth/MIME/size guards still intact)
Frontend — Login → /araclar → vehicle-menu-* dropdown → share-<carId> opens ShareCardModal
Frontend — share-format-row visible; share-format-classic / share-format-story_9_16 / share-format-square_1_1 all present
Frontend — ai-render-panel visible; ai-style-studio_dark / dramatic_lighting / billboard / showroom all present
Frontend — ai-render-btn + share-card-download-btn + share-card-copy-text-btn all present
Frontend — Format switching changes card aspect-ratio: 1080/1458 (Klasik) → 1080/1920 (Story 9:16) confirmed via style attribute introspection
Frontend — ai-render-btn correctly disabled when originalPhotoUrl is null (car without photo)
action_items: [Optional/Low] Add <DialogDescription> to ShareCardModal to silence Radix a11y warning

[Low] Verify sonner <Toaster /> mount + investigate why Metni Kopyala toast not surfacing in preview (likely clipboard write blocked — consider a document.execCommand('copy') fallback inside catch block, or a visible inline 'Kopyalandı ✓' badge instead of relying solely on sonner)
[None for iter57 scope] All AI render acceptance criteria met. No retest needed.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/ai_render.py — clean: MIME whitelist + 10MB cap + 1024B min, base64 encode → ImageContent → Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana) via emergentintegrations. STYLE_PRESETS in code dict + Pydantic Literal on Form param ⇒ double layer of validation (Pydantic 422 before route body). session_id uses uuid4 hex ⇒ no LLM session conflicts. 502 returned on LLM failure / empty image. Prompt explicitly preserves car identity (color/plate/wheels). No PII logging, no base64 leakage.
ai_render.py — STYLE_PRESETS.get(style) check at line 84 is unreachable (Literal already rejects unknowns at 422). Harmless defensive code, can stay.
ai_render.py — Server measured 24-31s per call vs PRD-stated 30-60s ⇒ within budget. Frontend toast.loading('AI render üretiliyor (~30-60 saniye)…') sets correct user expectation.

ShareCardModal.jsx — FORMAT_PRESETS cleanly drives aspect-ratio + maxWidth via inline style; verified DOM updates on click. AI_STYLES mirror backend keys exactly. ai-render-btn disabled={aiLoading || !originalPhotoUrl} ⇒ prevents wasteful LLM calls. originalPhotoUrl/photoUrl toggle pattern is correct (Orijinal button only shown when photoUrl !== originalPhotoUrl).

ShareCardModal.jsx:87 — handleDownload still uses alert() fallback ('Görsel oluşturulamadı.'). Inconsistent with rest of file which migrated to sonner toasts. Same at line 120. Cosmetic, low priority.
api.js aiRenderAPI.renderCar — 120s timeout is appropriate. Uses multipart/form-data correctly.
Minor: ImageContent and UserMessage emergentintegrations import re-validates universal LLM key contract. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY read at module-load and re-checked at request (500 if empty) ⇒ defensive.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seed. Test generates synthetic car-silhouette JPG in-memory via PIL (800x500, red sedan with cabin/wheels/headlights/road stripes ⇒ satisfies image_testing.md 'real visual features' rule, ~32KB). No mongo writes.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 57 ships AI render feature (POST /api/ai/render-car + ShareCardModal enhancements). Backend test: /app/backend/tests/test_iter57_ai_render.py — 6 tests, ~60s total (4 fast validation + 2 LLM calls @ 24-31s each). To rerun: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest backend/tests/test_iter57_ai_render.py -v -s. Per playbook: NEVER print full base64 (use [:20] head + length). LIMIT to 2 LLM calls per suite. Frontend selectors confirmed: share-format-{classic|story_9_16|square_1_1}, ai-render-panel, ai-style-{studio_dark|dramatic_lighting|billboard|showroom}, ai-render-btn, ai-render-revert-btn (only visible when photoUrl!==originalPhotoUrl), share-card-download-btn, share-card-copy-text-btn, share-card-smart-btn. To reach modal: login → /araclar → click vehicle-menu-<carId> → click share-<carId> dropdown item.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature validated end-to-end; no blocking bugs.
